setting.note: Rawi Hage's Beirut is the Christian East of the 1980s civil war: the tight streets of Achrafieh, the militia checkpoints and rooftop gunners, the port and the coast road north to the casino, all pressed against the Green Line that split the city from downtown south to the National Museum. The map runs from that dividing line across East Beirut to the harbour, then follows Bassam's flight out of the country.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Rawi Hage, first published 2006. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: De Niro's Game, Rawi Hage (2006). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The rust-red of shelled brick and dried blood that colours the war-torn city. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
